claims.claim: Adding peanut and egg in developmentally appropriate forms in the first year of life (6-12 months old) may reduce the risk of food allergy to these foods.
For toddlers 12 to 24 months old whose diets do not include meat, poultry, or seafood, the Committee advised providing eggs and dairy products on a regular basis.
Regular intake of other foods high in omega-3 fatty acids, such as... eggs that contain omega-3 fatty acids, is appropriate for those who do not consume seafood.
The Dietary Guidelines for Americans, 2020–2025 is grounded in the current body of scientific evidence on diet and health outcomes.
